scene: Scene 1 — Maya turns her finished study into a call for a firm
detail: The hero study card prints 'R6 — Attakar × Gura' twice (as the card title AND its single plan row, a render-bug smell); status is treated inconsistently within one card ('STUDY'/'Draft' are pills but 'reviewed' is plain grey text); and the card is stranded above ~250px of dead whitespace so the 'decluttered hero' reads as sparse rather than focused.
fix_recommendation: De-duplicate the study identity (show the study title once; label any plan row distinctly), render 'reviewed' as a semantic status pill consistent with the other badges, and let the candidate-plans section or a compact study gallery fill the canvas so the finished study reads as the focused hero. Product change, not a capture crop.

scene: Scene 1 — Maya turns her finished study into a call for a firm
detail: Legible to someone familiar with the space but a cold viewer stumbles: the card badges the study 'reviewed' while its inner plan row reads 'Draft', and the helper text introduces the orphan term 'bundle' which appears nowhere in the visible UI.
fix_recommendation: Resolve the status contradiction so the card states one unambiguous 'ready to solicit' state, and either show a bundle affordance or drop 'bundle' from the helper copy.

scene: Scene 1 — Maya turns her finished study into a call for a firm
detail: 'Compare selected' and '+ Group selected plans' read as actionable with no visible selection mechanism (no checkboxes), and the primary-button weight points at the filled 'Open study' rather than the scene's own concept action 'Create solicitation', which is the de-emphasized outline button.
fix_recommendation: Make selection affordances (checkboxes) visible on rows and disable the bulk buttons until a selection exists; promote 'Create solicitation' to the primary visual action on a finished study card.

scene: Scene 1 — Maya turns her finished study into a call for a firm
detail: The narrated 'finished study' is partially undercut on-screen by the visible 'Draft' plan row alongside the 'reviewed' label, so the viewer must trust the narration to reconcile the study's actual state. (Advisory; non-blocking.)
fix_recommendation: Align the on-card state language ('reviewed' / 'Draft') with the narration's 'finished study' so the footage backs the claim without a verbal bridge.

scene: Scene 1 — Maya turns her finished study into a call for a firm
detail: A mouse-cursor artifact is captured over the page title ('Microplan portfolio'), and a single static frame shows no affirmative smooth-transition evidence. No spinner, 404, or dead-end.
fix_recommendation: Capture without the cursor parked over the H1, and verify the Create-solicitation click transitions smoothly (no full reload/spinner) across scenes.

scene: Scene 2 — Maya finds the plan already waiting as the coverage area
detail: The load-bearing 'already attached, frozen at creation' confirmation is set in small secondary type in the lower third of a long create form, so the 5-second test yields only a partial read of the scene's core point.
fix_recommendation: Promote the 'Coverage areas (from micro-plan) / Attached' panel toward the top of the form (or pin a compact coverage summary near the title) so the frozen-plan confirmation is read first, not last.

scene: Scene 2 — Maya finds the plan already waiting as the coverage area
detail: The load-bearing coverage summary ('R6 — Attakar × Gura · 2 wards · 840 work areas · Study arms') is cramped into a small-type pill, and the map has no on-figure color->ward legend keying green=Attakar / blue=Gura, so the scene's color-coded wards are under-supported.
fix_recommendation: Give the coverage summary a larger, structured presentation — ward chips colored to match the map as an on-figure legend, work-area count as a stat — so the frozen coverage reads at a glance.

scene: Scene 3 — Amina reads the open call and ticks the area she can staff
detail: The promised two-way map<->checklist sync is not coherent in the captured frame: ticking the R6 checklist row produces NO visible selected-state on the corresponding map polygons (no fill/outline change), so the core 'click the map or tick below, synced' affordance is hand-waved and unverified.
fix_recommendation: When the R6 checklist row is checked, drive a visible selected state on the corresponding map polygon(s) — heavier outline + fill shift — so the sync the concept promises is observable.

scene: Scene 3 — Amina reads the open call and ticks the area she can staff
detail: The map shows two distinctly colored wards (Attakar green, Gura blue) but the checklist offers a single bundled 'R6 — Attakar × Gura' row, so a true outsider asks 'can I commit to one arm or only the combined block?'. The '×' notation is also cryptic.
fix_recommendation: Either split the checklist into per-area rows (Attakar / Gura) to match the two map colors, or add a one-line label clarifying 'Attakar × Gura = the combined R6 coverage block' so the single row reads intentionally.

scene: Scene 3 — Amina reads the open call and ticks the area she can staff
detail: The selected checklist row has only a faint blue background (weak active affordance), the Attakar/Gura area labels are tiny and low-contrast against competing basemap labels, and a mouse cursor is captured frozen over the checkbox.
fix_recommendation: Strengthen the selected-row affordance (border/checkmark + bolder fill), increase the weight/contrast of the Attakar/Gura area labels above basemap labels, and capture without the cursor over the control.

scene: Scene 3 — Amina reads the open call and ticks the area she can staff
detail: The narration's 'submission is blocked until one area is chosen' requires viewer trust: the captured frame already has the box ticked and Submit enabled, so the block itself is asserted, not shown. 'A specific coverage area' is also partially undercut by the single bundled row. (Advisory; non-blocking.)
fix_recommendation: Capture or add a prior frame with no area selected + Submit visibly disabled, so the block-until-selected mechanic is demonstrated rather than narrated.

scene: Scene 3 — Amina reads the open call and ticks the area she can staff
detail: A mouse-cursor glyph frozen over the checkbox mid-click reads as a slight hesitation cue, and the map shows no selected-state animation on tick, so the interaction doesn't read as fluid. No spinner/404/dead-end.
fix_recommendation: Ensure the click resolves cleanly (no lingering cursor) and that the map's selected-state animates on tick so the interaction reads as fluid.

scene: Scene 4 — Maya sees the firm tied to the coverage she designed
detail: A single-row table sits in a large card with heavy empty space below, and two of seven columns ('RECOMMENDATION' -> 'Not reviewed', 'SCORE' -> '-') render as hollow placeholders, so the comparison view reads as under-populated and slightly broken. Minor breadcrumb truncation / cursor occlusion. Real firm and contact names are grounding, not flaws.
fix_recommendation: Soften not-yet-applicable cells (lighter '—'), tighten the card to the loaded rows so one sparse row doesn't read as a broken table, and add a small '1 response' count. Do not crop the capture. (Upstream: seeding 2-3 responses would let the 'compare applicants' premise actually show.)

scene: Scene 5 — Maya awards the firm and sees it was notified
detail: The load-bearing 'Award budget: 500000' renders as a bare unformatted integer with no thousands separator and no currency symbol — the most important number on the screen forces the reader to mentally insert commas/currency. Compounded by an asymmetric two-column detail grid that leaves a large empty gap under the right column. Host chrome and the honest 'Next step — not yet built' panel are grounding, not flaws.
fix_recommendation: Format the budget as currency with separators (e.g. ₦500,000), and balance the detail grid so the right column does not leave a large empty gap.
